market_data.corners_note: Under 10.5 total corners flagged as value; no exact line retrieved
market_data.note: Market (63%) and model (73%) have a 10pp gap on Colombia win. Market gives more weight to draw and CDR. Blended used: 0.68.

rules_fired.RULE7_note: DR Congo 8.85% model win / 13.6% market win. Both below 15% threshold. RULE7 fires. Submit unshaded model estimates for UNDERDOG props. For Bakambu (losing-team striker): use 0.25-0.35 range per RULE15 revised <15% underdog framework.
rules_fired.RULE8_note: Draw 17.90% < 25%. RULE8 does NOT fire. No draw-shrinkage adjustment needed.
rules_fired.RULE15_note: Bakambu (Q10). DR Congo <15% win = full RULE15 suppression. Use 0.25-0.35 range regardless of club rate.
rules_fired.RULE_FOULS_note: Colombia has possession (56% in GD1). DR Congo defends (25% vs Portugal). Defending team fouls more = DR Congo fouls more direction. BUT: Colombia is a HIGH-PRESS team (11 fouls while attacking in GD1 — JPN-TUN lesson applies). Q4 estimate moderately NO but not aggressively.
rules_fired.FLOOR_0p25_note: Applied to Q10 (Bakambu SOT). Estimate 0.32 > floor.
rules_fired.RULE1_note: Q5 (Colombia win): avg(model 73.25%, market 62.7%) = 68%. Blended to 0.72 (model weighted slightly higher given RULE7 context).

group_standings_after_gd1.stakes_note: Colombia can clinch knockout round qualification with a win. DR Congo effectively must win — a draw only keeps them alive if Portugal drops points vs Uzbekistan.

colombia_gd1.key_note: Only 4 SOT from 15 shots (26.7% conversion). Luis Díaz: 1 goal + 1 assist + hit woodwork. First WC2026 player with goal+assist+woodwork in same match.

dr_congo_gd1.key_note: Held Portugal (dominant possession 68%) to only 1 total SOT in 7 shots — exceptional defensive efficiency. 0 goals in 2H despite being threatened. Bakambu started, had ~1 SOT (forced Costa save in 2H), rated 7/10. Two yellow cards: Bernardo Silva (POR), Mbemba (CDR).

luis_diaz.club: Liverpool FC / Bayern Munich (transferred 2025-26 per reports)
luis_diaz.sot_per_90_note: Exact figure not public; estimated from 13 PL goals in ~35 apps (~1.5-1.8 SOT/90)
luis_diaz.gd1_contributions: 1 goal (65') + 1 assist (Muñoz 40') + woodwork. 6 touches in opposition box. Only 2nd Colombian to score and assist in a WC match (after James vs Japan 2014).
luis_diaz.note: Colombia is the EXPECTED WINNER — RULE15 does not apply. RULE7 context for opponent (CDR <15% win) means Díaz will have MORE opportunities than usual.

cedric_bakambu.sot_per_90_note: From 561 min; primarily a sub role at Betis (19/25 apps from bench)
cedric_bakambu.gd1_details: 1 confirmed SOT (forced save from Diogo Costa in 2H). Had 2 total attempts. Rattled post (ruled foul in build-up). Fired over from cutback.
cedric_bakambu.rule15_framework: DR Congo <15% win probability (model 8.85%, market 13.6%). RULE15 revised framework: <15% underdog → use 0.25-0.35 range REGARDLESS of club SOT rate.
cedric_bakambu.note: FanDuel lists him at +490 scorer / +230 score-or-assist. Market presence confirmed — MAIN_BOOK_ABSENCE rule does not suppress further (he IS listed). Score-or-assist +230 implies ~30% for that prop; SOT should be 35-40% range, but RULE15 revised framework caps at 0.35 for <15% underdog.

referee_profile.name: Maurizio Mariani
referee_profile.penalty_note: Career rate 0.37/game HIGH; current season 0.24/game. Base P(penalty) ≈ 0.30. P(pen OR RC) ≈ 0.30 + 0.14 = 0.43 theoretical, but our historical pattern is to OVERPRICE this question type. Shade to 0.38.
referee_profile.card_note: High-card referee (4.81 career) but current season lower (3.95). Both teams relatively disciplined in GD1 (10-11 fouls each). However DR Congo defending desperately (RULE7) may trigger more cards.

bakambu_sot.rule15_revised_cap: 0.25-0.35 for <15% underdog (both model and market trigger this)
bakambu_sot.gd1_adjustment: 1 SOT confirmed in GD1 vs Portugal — above zero, supports being at 0.32 not 0.25

diaz_score_or_assist.gd1_form_boost: goal+assist in GD1; in excellent form
diaz_score_or_assist.rule7_context: CDR is extreme underdog; COL will dominate; more chances for Díaz

corners_col.note: Against compact DR Congo block, Colombia attacking heavily = corners generated. GD1 baseline 4; expect slightly more vs CDR's low block.

penalty_or_red_card.historical_overpricing_note: We have consistently overpriced this question type. Shade to 0.38.
penalty_or_red_card.rule7_upward_pressure: CDR defending desperately → more box fouls → higher penalty risk

Q1.question: Will Colombia have more shots on target than DR Congo in the second half?
key_drivers: Model: P(COL more 2H SOT) = 76.37%
RULE7 fires: CDR extreme underdog → Colombia will dominate SOT in 2H
Colombia GD1: 4 total SOT (2 expected in 2H); CDR GD1: 2 total SOT (1 expected in 2H)
CDR's compact 5-3-2 is excellent (held Portugal to 1 SOT total!) — slight shade from 76.37% down
BUT Colombia is the 73% favorite with λ_SOT ratio 3:1 → they should still dominate 2H SOT

Q2.question: Will Colombia score more goals than DR Congo in the second half?
key_drivers: Model: P(COL more 2H goals) = 50.67% (includes 0-0 in 2H = NO)
Colombia GD1: 2 goals in 2H (strong 2H scoring pattern)
DR Congo GD1: 0 goals in 2H vs Portugal (compact block held well in 2H)
Colombia's quality advantage → they score more when goals are scored
Market O/U 2.5 at +125/-155 suggests lower total goals → might reduce this estimate slightly

Q3.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown in the match?
key_drivers: Mariani current season: 0.24 pen/game, 0.14 RC/game → theoretical P(pen OR RC) ≈ 0.36
Both teams relatively disciplined (10-11 fouls in GD1)
RULE7 upward pressure: CDR defending desperately → more box fouls → penalty risk
HISTORICAL PATTERN: we consistently overprice this question type (ENG-GHA Q5 -4.09, others)
Shade from theoretical 0.36 slightly upward for RULE7 context: 0.38

Q4.question: Will Colombia commit more fouls than DR Congo?
key_drivers: RULE_FOULS: Colombia has possession → DR Congo defends → DR Congo fouls more direction
BUT: JPN-TUN LESSON — Colombia is a HIGH-PRESS team (11 fouls in GD1 while attacking!)
Colombia pressed aggressively vs Uzbekistan even with 56% possession
DR Congo GD1: only 10 fouls while defending vs Portugal (compact, disciplined block)
GD1 foul comparison: COL 11 vs CDR 10 — nearly identical. Near coin-flip.
RULE_FOULS direction gives slight NO lean: estimate 0.42

Q5.question: Will Colombia win the match?
key_drivers: Model: P(COL win) = 73.25%
Market: COL -195 → de-vigged ~62.7% win
Blended (model+market): ~68%; our estimate 0.72 slightly above blend given RULE7 context
RULE1: sharp market heavily weighted; DR Congo GD1 showed they can draw (1-1 vs Portugal) but are extreme underdogs
DR Congo effectively must win (need result if Portugal beats Uzbekistan) → they attack → might be open at back → Colombia on the break

Q6.question: At halftime, will Colombia be winning?
key_drivers: Model: P(COL leads at HT) = 50.67%
Colombia GD1: led at HT 1-0 vs Uzbekistan (Muñoz 40')
DR Congo GD1: 1-1 at HT vs Portugal (Wissa 45+5' equalizer)
CDR's compact 5-3-2 makes 1H opening difficult — they absorb pressure well
Colombia's λ_1H = 0.956 goals: P(COL scores 1H) ≈ 0.615; P(CDR scores 1H) ≈ 0.278
Slight YES lean given Colombia quality advantage; very close to coin-flip

Q7.question: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
key_drivers: Model: P(total ≤ 2) = 52.96%
Market: Under 2.5 at -155 → de-vigged 57-58% implied
DR Congo GD1: held Portugal to only 1 SOT in 7 shots — exceptional defensive discipline
Colombia GD1: 4 SOT from 15 shots (low conversion) — against compact CDR block, might struggle
Blended model/market estimate: 0.55-0.58. Settle at 0.56.

Q8.question: Will Colombia have 5 or more corner kicks?
key_drivers: Colombia GD1: 4 corners vs Uzbekistan (just below threshold)
Against compact CDR 5-3-2 low block, Colombia will generate more deflected shots → more corners
Market: Under 10.5 total corners flagged as value; DR Congo had only 4 corners vs Portugal
Corner rate vs deep defensive block tends to be higher (more deflections from blocked shots)
Near coin-flip: 0.48

Q9.question: Will Luis Díaz score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
key_drivers: FanDuel anytime scorer: +185 → 35.1% to score. Assist addon ~12%. Combined ~47%.
GD1: goal + assist (exceptional form — 2 contributions in 1 match)
RULE7 context: CDR extreme underdog → Colombia dominates → Díaz gets more chances than usual
CDR's 5-3-2 defensive block could limit his space on the left wing
No RULE15 (Colombia is the expected winner)
Estimate: 0.48 (market-anchored with slight upward adjustment for RULE7 + GD1 form)

Q10.question: Will Cédric Bakambu have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: RULE15 FIRES FULLY: DR Congo <15% win (model 8.85%, market 13.6%)
RULE15 revised framework: <15% underdog → use 0.25-0.35 range regardless of club rate
Club rate at Real Betis: 1.44 SOT/90 (La Liga), but primarily as a sub (19/25 apps off bench)
GD1: 1 confirmed SOT in 90 min vs Portugal (forced Costa save) — confirms he CAN get SOT
Market: FanDuel score/assist +230 (30.3% implied) → SOT probability somewhat higher (~35%)
Against Colombia (high-press 4-2-3-1 cutting off supply to CDR forwards), Bakambu isolated
FLOOR_0.25 confirmed as minimum. Estimate 0.32 (above floor; GD1 SOT warrants slight uplift from 0.25)

edge_ranking.note: Bakambu RULE15 full. CDR <15% win. Crowd likely ~0.43. Pattern from Semenyo (+15.14), Fajardo x2 confirmed.
Colombia win. Model 73% vs market 63%. Crowd likely ~0.67. Moderate YES edge.
Colombia more 2H SOT. Model 76.37%. CDR compact defense slight shade. Crowd ~0.65.
Penalty/red card. Historical overpricing. Crowd likely ~0.42.
≤2 total goals. Market Under -155 confirms. CDR defensive excellence.
Colombia more 2H goals. Colombia scored 2 in 2H in GD1; CDR scored 0 in 2H.
Colombia fouls. RULE_FOULS vs JPN-TUN high-press lesson. Near coin-flip.
Díaz score or assist. Market-anchored at ~47%. GD1 form excellent.
Colombia 5+ corners. GD1 baseline 4; compact CDR block adds corners. Coin-flip.
Colombia leads HT. Model 50.67%. GD1 led HT vs Uzbekistan. Coin-flip.

Q1_COL_more_2H_SOT.question: Will Colombia have more shots on target than DR Congo in the second half?

Q2_COL_more_2H_goals.question: Will Colombia score more goals than DR Congo in the second half?

Q3_penalty_or_red.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown in the match?

Q4_COL_more_fouls.question: Will Colombia commit more fouls than DR Congo?

Q6_HT_COL_winning.question: At halftime, will Colombia be winning?
Q6_HT_COL_winning.note: Colombia's eventual 1-0 winner came in the 2nd half — our slight YES lean on an early HT lead was wrong by a narrow margin.

Q7_2_or_fewer_goals.question: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?

Q8_COL_5plus_corners.question: Will Colombia have 5 or more corner kicks?
Q8_COL_5plus_corners.note: Flagged ourselves as 'near coin-flip' going in — the crowd's higher 58% (closer to actual YES) beat our 48%. Biggest loss of the match, from our lowest-conviction pick.

Q9_Diaz_score_or_assist.question: Will Luis Díaz score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?

Q10_Bakambu_SOT.question: Will Cédric Bakambu have at least 1 shot on target?
Q10_Bakambu_SOT.note: Biggest win of the match. RULE15 (extreme-underdog forward SOT suppression) confirmed cleanly against a much higher crowd estimate.

key_confirmations: Q10 (Bakambu, RULE15) was the standout win — extreme-underdog (DR Congo <15% win prob) forward SOT suppression continues to be one of the most reliable rules in the whole tournament.

Q8 (corners) was our only real miss, and it was the one question we explicitly flagged as a coin-flip going in — confirms that flagged low-conviction picks are exactly where crowd-beating edge disappears.
